violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Observed bag of flour on floor in storage area.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Observed that nothing was date marked in cold hold unit, or in 7-Up cooler.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --An approved sanitizer was not used during three compartment sink operation. Observed container of quat and test strips by three compartment sink, but owner stated they were using Clorox wipes to sanitize cook ware. Explained that they are not an approved sanitizer.
Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in the proper location to protect from contamination. -Observed ladle in coleslaw container in 7-Up cooler.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Observed bare wood on counters of display area.
Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. -Observed hand sink had no soap at time of inspection. COS.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room was not clean or kept in good repair. -Observed mechanical ventilation unit was dirty and had dust in vents.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)were not maintained in good repair. -Observed cracked and broken floor tiles in food prep area.
Lighting. — --Light intensity for walk-in refrigeration or food storage area was insufficient. -Observed walk in freezer had no light bulb.
